Version: 1.0

Library Method™

Concept_Type:
methodology

Category: knowledge framework

Identifier: librarymethod-framework-v1

First_Publication: 2026-02-01

Canonical: https://www.librarymethod.com

Creator: Adrian Wise Santos
Publisher: GIGO Data, Inc.

Status:
public reference implementation

Purpose: To establish verifiable professional history through structured
capture, classification, verification, and retrieval of work records.

Description: Library Method™ is a structured methodology for capturing, classifying,
verifying, and retrieving work history as defensible evidence.

Definition: Library Method™ is a structured methodology for capturing,
classifying, verifying, and retrieving professional work history
as verifiable evidence. The framework establishes provenance, narrative coherence, and trust in historical records used by individuals, organisations, and AI systems.

Topics:
knowledge systems
knowledge provenance
epistemology of data
evidence-based work history
professional narrative reconstruction
structured career documentation
verifiable work history
work history verification
data trust systems
AI governance
AI accountability
human-centered data systems
knowledge cataloguing
professional identity systems
career provenance
historical record integrity
information architecture
documentation systems
evidence-based resumes
structured professional memory
organizational memory
data lineage
trustworthy AI inputs

Domains:
career systems
AI governance

data provenance

organizational knowledge management

Related_Concepts:

data lineage
